contentBlocks.original:
माणिक्यं –
ततो रावणनीतायाः सीतायाः शत्रुकर्शनः ।
इयेष पदमन्वेष्टुं चारणाचरिते पथि ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
māṇikyaṃ –
tatō rāvaṇanītāyāḥ sītāyāḥ śatrukarśanaḥ ।
iyēṣa padamanvēṣṭuṃ chāraṇācharitē pathi ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Ruby — Māṇikya] Then Rāma, the tormentor of enemies, desired to find the path trodden by the celestial singers (cāraṇas) — the path that Sītā, carried away by Rāvaṇa, had traveled. [This verse, praising Hanumān by celebrating the Rāmāyaṇa, is the ruby gem of this garland.]
contentBlocks.meanings: māṇikya: ruby (first gem) | tataḥ: then | rāvaṇa-nītāyāḥ: of Sītā who was carried away by Rāvaṇa | sītāyāḥ: Sītā's | śatru-karśanaḥ: the tormentor of enemies (Rāma) | iyeṣa: desired | padam: path | anveṣṭum: to find | cāraṇa-ācarite: trodden by the cāraṇas (celestial singers/witnesses) | pathi: on the path

contentBlocks.original:
मुत्यं –
यस्य त्वेतानि चत्वारि वानरेन्द्र यथा तव ।
स्मृतिर्मतिर्धृतिर्दाक्ष्यं स कर्मसु न सीदति ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mutyaṃ –
yasya tvētāni chatvāri vānarēndra yathā tava ।
smṛtirmatirdhṛtirdākṣyaṃ sa karmasu na sīdati ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Pearl — Mutya] O king of monkeys — whoever has these four qualities as you do: memory (smṛti), wisdom (mati), steadfastness (dhṛti), and skill (dākṣya) — he does not fail in any undertaking.
contentBlocks.meanings: mutya: pearl (second gem) | yasya tv etāni catvāri: whoever has these four | vānara-indra: O king of monkeys | yathā tava: as you have | smṛtiḥ: memory | matiḥ: wisdom/intelligence | dhṛtiḥ: steadfastness/courage | dākṣyam: skill/dexterity | saḥ karmasv na sīdati: he does not fail in any undertaking

contentBlocks.original:
प्रवालं –
अनिर्वेदः श्रियो मूलं अनिर्वेदः परं सुखम् ।
अनिर्वेदो हि सततं सर्वार्थेषु प्रवर्तकः ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pravālaṃ –
anirvēdaḥ śriyō mūlaṃ anirvēdaḥ paraṃ sukham ।
anirvēdō hi satataṃ sarvārthēṣu pravartakaḥ ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Coral — Pravāla] Non-dejection (anirvedaḥ) is the root of prosperity. Non-dejection is the supreme happiness. Non-dejection indeed continuously prevails in all purposes. Therefore non-dejection should be cultivated in every way.
contentBlocks.meanings: pravāla: coral (third gem) | anirvedaḥ: non-dejection/non-despair | śriyaḥ mūlam: root of prosperity | parama sukham: supreme happiness | sarvārthaṣu pravartatē: prevails in all purposes | ataḥ: therefore | anirvedaḥ: non-dejection | sevyo: should be cultivated | sarvathā: in every way

contentBlocks.original:
मरकतं –
नमोऽस्तु रामाय सलक्ष्मणाय
देव्यै च तस्यै जनकात्मजायै ।
नमोऽस्तु रुद्रेन्द्रयमानिलेभ्यः
नमोऽस्तु चन्द्रार्कमरुद्गणेभ्यः ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
marakataṃ –
namō'stu rāmāya salakṣmaṇāya
dēvyai cha tasyai janakātmajāyai ।
namō'stu rudrēndrayamānilēbhyaḥ
namō'stu chandrārkamarudgaṇēbhyaḥ ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Emerald — Marakata] Salutations to Rāma together with Lakṣmaṇa, and to the Goddess Sītā, daughter of Janaka. Salutations to Rudra, Indra, Yama, Varuṇa, the wind-god — salutations to the Moon, the two Aśvins, and the Sun. Salutations to Brahmā and all the gods.
contentBlocks.meanings: marakata: emerald (fourth gem) | namo'stu rāmāya sa-lakṣmaṇāya: salutations to Rāma together with Lakṣmaṇa | devyai ca tasyai janak-ātmajāyai: and to the goddess, daughter of Janaka (Sītā) | namo'stu rudrendra-yama-varuṇebhyaḥ: salutations to Rudra, Indra, Yama, Varuṇa | namo'stu candra-dvita-sūryebhyaḥ: salutations to Moon, the two Aśvins, and the Sun | namo'stu brahmādi-surebhyaḥ: salutations to Brahmā and all gods

contentBlocks.original:
पुष्यरागं –
प्रियान्न सम्भवेद्दुःखं अप्रियादधिकं भयम् ।
ताभ्यां हि ये वियुज्यन्ते नमस्तेषां महात्मनाम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
puṣyarāgaṃ –
priyānna sambhavēdduḥkhaṃ apriyādadhikaṃ bhayam ।
tābhyāṃ hi yē viyujyantē namastēṣāṃ mahātmanām ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Yellow Topaz — Puṣyarāga] From the dear (priya) there cannot arise suffering. From the unpleasant (apriya) there is greater fear. For those who are separated from both of these — salutations to those people of equanimity.
contentBlocks.meanings: puṣyarāga: yellow topaz (fifth gem) | priyān na sambhavet duḥkham: from the dear/pleasant, suffering cannot arise | apriyād adhikaṃ bhayam: from the unpleasant, greater fear arises | tābhyāṃ hi ye viyujyante: for those who are separated from both of these | namas teṣāṃ: salutations to those people

contentBlocks.original:
हीरकं –
रामः कमलपत्राक्षः सर्वसत्त्वमनोहरः ।
रूपदाक्षिण्यसम्पन्नः प्रसूतो जनकात्मजे ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hīrakaṃ –
rāmaḥ kamalapatrākṣaḥ sarvasattvamanōharaḥ ।
rūpadākṣiṇyasampannaḥ prasūtō janakātmajē ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Diamond — Hīraka] Rāma — of lotus-petal eyes — captivator of the minds of all beings — endowed with beauty and courtesy — was born to the daughter of Janaka [as her husband].
contentBlocks.meanings: hīraka: diamond (sixth gem) | rāmaḥ: Rāma | kamala-patra-akṣaḥ: of lotus-petal eyes | sarva-sattva-mano-haraḥ: captivator of the minds of all beings | rūpa-dākṣiṇya-sampannaḥ: endowed with beauty and gracious courtesy | prasūtaḥ: born to/appeared for | janak-ātmaje: the daughter of Janaka (Sītā)

contentBlocks.original:
इन्द्रनीलं –
जयत्यतिबलो रामो लक्ष्मणश्च महाबलः ।
राजा जयति सुग्रीवो राघवेणाभिपालितः ।
दासोऽहं कोसलेन्द्रस्य रामस्याक्लिष्टकर्मणः ।
हनुमान् शत्रुसैन्यानां निहन्ता मारुतात्मजः ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
indranīlaṃ –
jayatyatibalō rāmō lakṣmaṇaścha mahābalaḥ ।
rājā jayati sugrīvō rāghavēṇābhipālitaḥ ।
dāsō'haṃ kōsalēndrasya rāmasyākliṣṭakarmaṇaḥ ।
hanumān śatrusainyānāṃ nihantā mārutātmajaḥ ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Sapphire — Indranīla] Victory to the supremely strong Rāma and the mighty Lakṣmaṇa. May King Sugrīva be victorious — protected by Rāghava. And may Daśagrīva (Rāvaṇa) be defeated.
contentBlocks.meanings: indranīla: sapphire/indigo gem (seventh gem) | jayati ati-balo rāmaḥ: victory to the supremely strong Rāma | lakṣmaṇaś ca mahā-balaḥ: and the mighty Lakṣmaṇa | rājā jayati sugrīvaḥ: may king Sugrīva be victorious | rāghaveṇa abhipālitaḥ: protected by Rāghava | daśagrīvo hataḥ: may Daśagrīva (ten-necked Rāvaṇa) be defeated

contentBlocks.original:
गोमेधिकं –
यद्यस्ति पतिशुश्रूषा यद्यस्ति चरितं तपः ।
यदि वास्त्येकपत्नीत्वं शीतो भव हनूमतः ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gōmēdhikaṃ –
yadyasti patiśuśrūṣā yadyasti charitaṃ tapaḥ ।
yadi vāstyēkapatnītvaṃ śītō bhava hanūmataḥ ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Hessonite — Gomedha] If there is devoted service to a husband, if there has been virtuous conduct and austerity, and if there is the virtue of devotion to one husband — O Hanumān, be cool/pacified [by this truth, as Sītā tested you].
contentBlocks.meanings: gomedha: hessonite/garnet (eighth gem) | yady asti pati-śuśrūṣā: if there is devoted service to a husband | yady asti caritam tapaḥ: if there has been virtuous conduct and austerity | yadi vā'sty eka-patnītvaṃ: if there is the virtue of devotion to one husband (pativratā dharma) | śīto bhava hanūmataḥ: O Hanumān, be cool

contentBlocks.original:
वैडूर्यं –
निवृत्तवनवासं तं त्वया सार्धमरिन्दमम् ।
अभिषिक्तमयोध्यायां क्षिप्रं द्रक्ष्यसि राघवम् ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vaiḍūryaṃ –
nivṛttavanavāsaṃ taṃ tvayā sārdhamarindamam ।
abhiṣiktamayōdhyāyāṃ kṣipraṃ drakṣyasi rāghavam ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Cat's Eye — Vaiḍūrya] You will soon see the one who has returned from forest exile — together with you, the conqueror of enemies — consecrated in Ayodhyā — after the enemies have been destroyed.
contentBlocks.meanings: vaiḍūrya: cat's eye gem (ninth gem) | nivṛtta-vana-vāsaṃ tam: the one who has returned from forest exile | tvayā sārdham: together with you | arim-damam: the conqueror of enemies | abhiṣiktam ayodhyāyāṃ: consecrated/crowned in Ayodhyā | kṣipraṃ drakṣyasi: you will soon see

contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the Śrī Añjaneya Navaratna Mālā Stotram.
contentBlocks.meanings: iti: thus ends | śrī āñjaneya: of Śrī Añjaneya (Hanumān) | navaratna-mālā stotram: the nine-gem garland hymn
